mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth signals robust corporate earnings potential, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through improved cash flow expectations.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods of INDPRO growth remain below 1.5% with statistical insignificance (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in unemployment rate below 4.0% indicates tightening labor market conditions that boost consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in triggered periods show negative or flat performance (return < 0.5%) with p > 0.05 in t-test against baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and inflation (CPIAUCSL) indicates strong aggregate demand, creating positive equity return momentum through elevated corporate pricing power.
failure_condition: Joint growth in GDPC1 and CPIAUCSL fails to produce positive 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of cases (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates above 2.0% signal monetary tightening that increases discount rates, leading to negative S&P 500 returns during 60-day horizons.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in high-rate regimes exceed 5% annually with p < 0.05 in positive performance tests.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) indicates sustained employment expansion that drives consumer confidence and equity valuations, producing positive 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 5-day SPX returns in PAYEMS growth periods show negative performance (return < -1%) with p > 0.10 in t-test.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) exceeding 1,300 thousand units signal strong demand for construction and related sectors, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in HOUST growth periods remain below 0.8% with p > 0.05 in performance tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) and inflation (CPIAUCSL) create stagflationary pressures that depress equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: Joint FEDFUNDS/CPIAUCSL increases fail to produce negative 60-day SPX returns in > 50% of cases (Rank IC > -0.05).

mechanism_narrative: High consumer spending (RSAFS) combined with rising housing starts (HOUST) indicates robust demand conditions that drive S&P 500 positive returns during 5-day horizons.
failure_condition: RSAFS/HOUST co-movements fail to generate positive 5-day SPX returns in > 55% of cases (Rank IC < 0.03).
